content_en:
A: Based on the fact that the father made the gift in the dower of his son’s wife, he can in no way return it.

BEQUESTS A bequest or an acknowledgment of a debt?

Q: A man in his death illness said: “So and so sum of money should be paid to the orphans of such and such. It was not clear whether his saying was intended to be an acknowledgement of a debt he owed or a bequest?

A: If there was an indication in the context whether he intended an acknowledgement of a debt or a bequest, the deceased’s phrase should be interpreted in the light of such indication. If it cannot be distinguished, it will be considered as a bequest.

Making bequests of unequal shares to one’s children

Q: A man bequeathed unequal shares to his children and brought witnesses to attest such bequest in his deathbed. Can this bequest be effective or not?

A: An ill person is not permitted to assign a bequest to some of his children, to be delivered in his lifetime or after his death, nor to acknowledge a sum of money or property to one of his children. should he does an of the above, it will not be effective, unless after the consent of the remaining heirs. This is according to the consensus of Muslim scholars. Being a witness on such kind of bequests is not permissible, as it is regarded as supporting an unfair cause. Such kind of bequests or gifts is considered one of the greatest sins that bring about destruction to it their doer. This is because a bequest or a gift of such kind most likely leads to disagreement and even hatred and enmity among the heirs.

Q: A woman. bequeathed one-third of her property to her daughter before her death. The father of the girl then was made her guardian. The father then made a case at the court to prove such bequest. The court ascertained the death of the testatrix, her being bequeathed to her daughter, and that her father accepted to be a guardian of such bequest. The judge, however, could not issue a judgment to prove the bequest because of the inability to let the young girl make an oath to this effect due to her minority. Should her father take an oath instead of her? Or should the judge postpone the issuance of the judgment until the girl attains majority to take the oath herself?

A: The father should not take the oath instead of her daughter, nor should the judgment be postponed until the girl attains majority. Rather, the judgment should be issued immediately. There is not disagreement among scholars on this point, so long as no legal claim is made against the father that opposes his claim.

Annulment of a bequest

Q: A woman made a bequest in her illness to certain persons, including her husband and her brother. Long after, she gave birth to a male child, then died. Can the bequest she made be annulled in this case?

A: All that exceeds one-third of the bequest should be returned to the heirs. As for the bequest for the husband, it is not valid, as he is entitled to inheritance. As for the brother, the bequest for him is valid, as he cannot inherit in the presence of the son. Thus, the portion bequeathed to the brother and others should not exceed one-third of the whole estate, and it is to be distributed among them according to the shares specified by the deceased.

Can a nephew be an heir?

Q: A woman died and left behind a nephew (son of her sister). She allocated more than one-third of her estate as a charity. Can this bequest be effected, thus giving the nephew the rest of the estate?

A: A bequest should not exceed one-third out of the entire estate. More than one-third is not permitted, unless after the consent of the legal heirs. A nephew is entitled to inherit the entire estate, according to the opinion of the scholars who give the uterine relatives the right to inherit. This is the opinion of the majority of early Muslims (Salaf), Abu Hanifah, Ahmad, a group of the Shafi’i scholars, and a variant opinion in the Maliki School, in case the public treasury is deficient.

A bequest to a husband and a paternal uncle and a grandmother

Q: A woman died and left behind a father, a paternal uncle and a grandmother. He father had claimed her majority at the court before he gave her in marriage. In her death illness, she bequeathed one-half of her estate to her husband and the other one-half to her paternal uncle, giving nothing to her father and grandmother. Is this bequest valid?

A: The bequest for the paternal uncle is valid, but should not exceed one-third, except with the consent of other heirs. The bequest for the husband, however, is totally invalid, except if the heirs accepted it. If the heirs rejected the bequest of more than one-third to the paternal uncle, the husband will be entitled to half of the estate after deducting the portion bequeathed, i.e. one-third. The grandmother and the father will be entitled to one-sixth each.

A bequest of Hajj

Q: Five days prior to her death, a woman bequeathed some things, including Hajj, recitation of the Holy Qur’an. charity, etc. Is her bequest applicable?

A: If she bequeathed that one-third of her estate he dedicated to finance certain acts of devotion to pleasc

Allah, her bequest should be fulfilled, even in her death illness. If the bequest exceeds one-third of the whole estate, the increase should be excluded, except if the heirs consent. If the bequest is made for anything other than the acts of devotion, it will not be executable.

Benefit for the deceased

Q: A man bequeathed before his death that his wife should not give money to whomever recites Qur’an on his grave or recite Qur’anic verses and then dedicate them to him, claiming that his heart was already full of Qur’an and in need of no more. The wife knew that he could have not memorized the Holy Qur’an. Can his bequest be effective? The wife’s purpose is to give money to any needy person in return for reciting Qur’anic verses and dedicating them to her husband. Is she permitted to do so?

A: The bequest of the husband should be fulfilled, since the gratuity in return for recitation of the Qur’an on the grave ina religious innovation that should be avoided. It is only acceptable if made for free. This can be elaborated as follows:

1. Hiring someone to recite Qur’anic verses and

dedicate them to the deceased is not acceptable, and no reward of that will reach the deceased.

2. Hiring someone to recite Qur’anic verses only is also a religious innovation.

3. Hiring someone as an _ instructor of the Holy Qur’an is permissible.

If that woman desires to benefit her deceased husband, she may give charity on his behalf, as the reward of charity reaches the deceased, according to the unanimous agreement of scholars. If she gives charity to a group of poor reciters to fulfill their needs and enable them to give up the profession of reciting Qur’an on the graves, her charity will be rewardable and such reward will reach the deceased.

The guardian of the orphan girl

Q: An orphan girl has a wealth, and a person is proposing to her. Can her guardian sell some of her property to buy her the requirements of marriage that suits her social status?

A: Yes, the guardian can sell some of her property to buy her all necessities of the wedding on equitable terms.

THERIFANCE The share of the widow

Q: What is the share of the widow of the inheritance of her deceased husband, knowing that he left behind children?

A: The widow is entitled to her dower and all liabilities the husband owed her, just like other creditors. After deducting the amounts of debt and executable bequest out of the total of the estate, the wife is entitled to one-eighth of the estate due to the presence of the children.

The share of the husband from the inheritance of his deceased wife

Q: A woman died leaving behind a husband and two parents. The father seized her estate, claiming that his deceased daughter was not major. Is the husband entitled to inherit her?

A: The estate of the deceased wife is to be distributed as follows:

- One-half for the husband,

- One third for the father, and

- One-sixth for the mother. This is according to the view of the Four Juristic Schools, no matter she was major or not.

How to distribute the estate?

Q: Give each of the following heirs his/her share of inheritance: A husband, grandmother, full-brothers and a son?

- The husband is entitled to one-fourth,

- The grandmother is entitled to one-sixth,

- The son is entitled to the remaining portion of the estate,

- The full brothers receive nothing.

The sisters and the daughters

Q: A woman died leaving behind a husband, two daughters, a mother, and two full-sisters. Are the sisters entitled to a share of inheritance?

A: the question is to be divided as follows:

- The husband is entitled to one-fourth,

- The mother is entitled to one-sixth,

- The two daughters are entitled to two-thirds,

- The full-sisters will receive nothing in the presence of the daughters. This is according to the consensus of the Four Juristic Schools.

Paternal and maternal brothers and sisters

Q: A woman died leaving behind a husband, mother, fullsister and a_ paternal brother and sister. Give each his/her share of inheritance?

A: The question consists of ten shares, originally six, but turns to ten by virtue of the application of the scheme of distribution called ‘Awl. The question is called “Dhat AlFurukh” [the Mother of the Chicks,] due to the so many resort to the scheme of ‘Awl in it.

- The husband is entitled to half of the estate,

- The mother is entitled to one-sixth as a share,

- The full-sister is entitled to three shares,

- The paternal sister is entitled to one-sixth in order to complete the share of the two-thirds with the fullsister. The two maternal brother and sister are entitled to one-third as two shares. The total thus is ten shares. This is according to the consensus of the Muslim scholars.

A husband, mother and maternal sister

Q: A woman died leaving behind a husband, mother, and maternal sister. What is the share of each of inheritance?

A: This question is to be divided into eleven shares:

- The daughter is entitled six shares,
content_id:
J: Berdasarkan fakta bahwa ayah memberikan hadiah tersebut sebagai mahar istri putranya, ia sama sekali tidak dapat mengambilnya kembali.

WASIAT Wasiat atau pengakuan hutang?

T: Seorang laki-laki dalam sakit menjelang wafatnya berkata: "Sejumlah uang tertentu harus dibayarkan kepada anak-anak yatim si fulan." Tidak jelas apakah ucapannya tersebut dimaksudkan sebagai pengakuan atas hutang yang ia miliki ataukah sebagai wasiat?

J: Jika terdapat petunjuk dalam konteks tersebut apakah ia bermaksud mengakui hutang atau berwasiat, maka ungkapan almarhum harus ditafsirkan berdasarkan petunjuk tersebut. Jika tidak dapat dibedakan, maka hal itu dianggap sebagai wasiat.

Berwasiat dengan bagian yang tidak sama kepada anak-anaknya

T: Seorang laki-laki mewasiatkan bagian yang tidak sama kepada anak-anaknya dan mendatangkan saksi-saksi untuk membuktikan wasiat tersebut di ranjang kematiannya. Apakah wasiat ini dapat berlaku atau tidak?

J: Orang yang sakit tidak diperbolehkan memberikan wasiat kepada sebagian anaknya, baik diserahkan semasa hidupnya maupun setelah wafatnya, dan tidak pula diperbolehkan mengakui sejumlah uang atau harta kepada salah seorang anaknya. Jika ia melakukan salah satu dari hal-hal di atas, maka hal itu tidak berlaku, kecuali setelah mendapat persetujuan dari ahli waris lainnya. Hal ini berdasarkan ijma' ulama Muslim. Menjadi saksi atas jenis wasiat semacam ini tidak diperbolehkan, karena dianggap sebagai mendukung perkara yang tidak adil. Jenis wasiat atau hibah semacam ini termasuk salah satu dosa terbesar yang mendatangkan kehancuran bagi pelakunya. Hal ini karena wasiat atau hibah semacam itu kemungkinan besar menimbulkan perselisihan bahkan kebencian dan permusuhan di antara para ahli waris.

T: Seorang perempuan mewasiatkan sepertiga hartanya kepada putrinya sebelum wafatnya. Ayah sang anak kemudian dijadikan walinya. Sang ayah kemudian mengajukan perkara ke pengadilan untuk membuktikan wasiat tersebut. Pengadilan memastikan wafatnya pewasiat, bahwa ia telah berwasiat kepada putrinya, dan bahwa ayahnya menerima untuk menjadi wali atas wasiat tersebut. Namun hakim tidak dapat mengeluarkan putusan untuk membuktikan wasiat tersebut karena ketidakmampuan membuat anak perempuan yang masih kecil itu bersumpah terkait hal ini disebabkan belum mencapai usia baligh. Apakah ayahnya harus bersumpah menggantikannya? Ataukah hakim harus menunda pengeluaran putusan sampai anak perempuan itu mencapai usia baligh untuk bersumpah sendiri?

J: Ayah tidak boleh bersumpah menggantikan putrinya, dan putusan tidak boleh ditunda sampai anak perempuan itu mencapai usia baligh. Sebaliknya, putusan harus segera dikeluarkan. Tidak ada perbedaan pendapat di antara ulama mengenai hal ini, selama tidak ada tuntutan hukum terhadap ayah yang bertentangan dengan klaimnya.

Pembatalan wasiat

T: Seorang perempuan membuat wasiat dalam masa sakitnya kepada orang-orang tertentu, termasuk suami dan saudaranya. Lama setelah itu, ia melahirkan seorang anak laki-laki, kemudian wafat. Apakah wasiat yang ia buat dapat dibatalkan dalam kasus ini?

J: Semua yang melebihi sepertiga dari wasiat harus dikembalikan kepada para ahli waris. Adapun wasiat untuk suami, tidak sah karena ia berhak mendapat warisan. Adapun saudara laki-lakinya, wasiat untuknya sah karena ia tidak dapat mewarisi dengan adanya anak laki-laki. Dengan demikian, bagian yang diwasiatkan kepada saudara laki-laki dan lainnya tidak boleh melebihi sepertiga dari seluruh harta peninggalan, dan dibagikan di antara mereka sesuai bagian yang ditentukan oleh almarhum.

Apakah keponakan dapat menjadi ahli waris?

T: Seorang perempuan wafat dan meninggalkan seorang keponakan (putra saudara perempuannya). Ia menetapkan lebih dari sepertiga hartanya sebagai sedekah. Apakah wasiat ini dapat dilaksanakan, sehingga keponakan mendapatkan sisa hartanya?

J: Wasiat tidak boleh melebihi sepertiga dari seluruh harta peninggalan. Lebih dari sepertiga tidak diperbolehkan, kecuali setelah mendapat persetujuan ahli waris yang sah. Keponakan berhak mewarisi seluruh harta peninggalan, menurut pendapat ulama yang memberikan hak waris kepada kerabat dari pihak ibu. Ini adalah pendapat mayoritas ulama Salaf, Abu Hanifah, Ahmad, sekelompok ulama Syafi'i, dan pendapat lain dalam madzhab Maliki, apabila kas negara tidak mencukupi.

Wasiat untuk suami, paman dari pihak ayah, dan nenek

T: Seorang perempuan wafat dan meninggalkan seorang ayah, seorang paman dari pihak ayah, dan seorang nenek. Ayahnya telah mengklaim kedewasaannya di pengadilan sebelum menikahkannya. Dalam sakit menjelang wafatnya, ia mewasiatkan setengah hartanya untuk suaminya dan setengah lainnya untuk paman dari pihak ayahnya, tanpa memberikan apa pun kepada ayah dan neneknya. Apakah wasiat ini sah?

J: Wasiat untuk paman dari pihak ayah adalah sah, namun tidak boleh melebihi sepertiga, kecuali dengan persetujuan ahli waris lainnya. Adapun wasiat untuk suami sama sekali tidak sah, kecuali jika para ahli waris menerimanya. Jika para ahli waris menolak wasiat lebih dari sepertiga untuk paman dari pihak ayah, maka suami berhak mendapat setengah dari harta peninggalan setelah dikurangi bagian yang diwasiatkan, yaitu sepertiga. Nenek dan ayah masing-masing berhak mendapat seperenam.

Wasiat Hajj

T: Lima hari sebelum wafatnya, seorang perempuan mewasiatkan beberapa hal, termasuk Hajj, pembacaan Al-Quran, sedekah, dan sebagainya. Apakah wasiatnya dapat dilaksanakan?

J: Jika ia mewasiatkan agar sepertiga dari hartanya didedikasikan untuk membiayai amal-amal ibadah tertentu guna meraih ridha

Allah, maka wasiatnya harus dipenuhi, meskipun dalam sakit menjelang wafatnya. Jika wasiat melebihi sepertiga dari seluruh harta peninggalan, maka kelebihannya harus dikecualikan, kecuali jika para ahli waris menyetujuinya. Jika wasiat dibuat untuk selain amal ibadah, maka tidak dapat dilaksanakan.

Manfaat bagi almarhum

T: Seorang laki-laki mewasiatkan sebelum wafatnya agar istrinya tidak memberikan uang kepada siapa pun yang membacakan Al-Quran di kuburannya atau membacakan ayat-ayat Al-Quran lalu menghadiahkannya kepadanya, dengan alasan bahwa hatinya sudah penuh dengan Al-Quran dan tidak membutuhkan lagi. Sang istri mengetahui bahwa ia tidak mungkin telah menghafal Al-Quran. Apakah wasiatnya dapat berlaku? Maksud sang istri adalah memberikan uang kepada orang yang membutuhkan sebagai imbalan atas pembacaan ayat-ayat Al-Quran dan menghadiahkannya kepada suaminya. Apakah ia diperbolehkan melakukan hal itu?

J: Wasiat suami harus dipenuhi, karena memberikan imbalan atas pembacaan Al-Quran di kuburan adalah bid'ah yang harus dihindari. Hal itu hanya dapat diterima jika dilakukan secara sukarela. Hal ini dapat dijelaskan sebagai berikut:

1. Menyewa seseorang untuk membacakan ayat-ayat Al-Quran dan

menghadiahkannya kepada almarhum adalah tidak dapat diterima, dan pahalanya tidak akan sampai kepada almarhum.

2. Menyewa seseorang untuk membacakan ayat-ayat Al-Quran saja juga merupakan bid'ah.

3. Menyewa seseorang sebagai pengajar Al-Quran adalah diperbolehkan.

Jika perempuan itu ingin memberikan manfaat kepada suaminya yang telah wafat, ia dapat bersedekah atas namanya, karena pahala sedekah sampai kepada almarhum, berdasarkan kesepakatan ulama. Jika ia bersedekah kepada sekelompok pembaca Al-Quran yang fakir untuk memenuhi kebutuhan mereka dan memungkinkan mereka meninggalkan profesi membaca Al-Quran di kuburan, maka sedekahnya akan mendapat pahala dan pahala tersebut akan sampai kepada almarhum.

Wali anak perempuan yatim

T: Seorang anak perempuan yatim memiliki harta, dan seseorang melamarnya. Apakah walinya dapat menjual sebagian hartanya untuk membeli keperluannya dalam pernikahan yang sesuai dengan status sosialnya?

J: Ya, wali dapat menjual sebagian hartanya untuk membeli semua keperluan pernikahan dengan syarat yang adil.

WARISAN Bagian janda

T: Berapa bagian janda dari warisan suaminya yang telah wafat, mengingat ia meninggalkan anak-anak?

J: Janda berhak mendapatkan maharnya dan semua kewajiban yang suami miliki kepadanya, sama seperti kreditur lainnya. Setelah dikurangi jumlah hutang dan wasiat yang dapat dilaksanakan dari total harta peninggalan, istri berhak mendapat seperdelapan dari harta peninggalan karena adanya anak-anak.

Bagian suami dari warisan istrinya yang telah wafat

T: Seorang perempuan wafat meninggalkan suami dan kedua orang tua. Sang ayah menyita harta peninggalannya, dengan mengklaim bahwa putrinya yang telah wafat itu belum dewasa. Apakah suami berhak mewarisinya?

J: Harta peninggalan istri yang telah wafat dibagikan sebagai berikut:

- Setengah untuk suami,

- Sepertiga untuk ayah, dan

- Seperenam untuk ibu. Hal ini sesuai dengan pandangan Empat Madzhab Fiqh, baik ia sudah dewasa maupun belum.

Bagaimana cara membagi harta peninggalan?

T: Berikan masing-masing ahli waris berikut bagian warisannya: Suami, nenek, saudara laki-laki kandung, dan seorang anak laki-laki?

- Suami berhak mendapat seperempat,

- Nenek berhak mendapat seperenam,

- Anak laki-laki berhak atas sisa bagian harta warisan,

- Saudara-saudara kandung laki-laki tidak mendapat bagian.

Saudara perempuan dan anak perempuan

T: Seorang wanita meninggal dunia meninggalkan seorang suami, dua anak perempuan, seorang ibu, dan dua saudara perempuan kandung. Apakah saudara-saudara perempuan tersebut berhak mendapat bagian warisan?

J: Pertanyaan ini dibagi sebagai berikut:

- Suami berhak atas seperempat,

- Ibu berhak atas seperenam,

- Dua anak perempuan berhak atas dua pertiga,

- Saudara-saudara perempuan kandung tidak mendapat bagian dengan adanya anak perempuan. Ini adalah berdasarkan ijma' Empat Madzhab Fiqh.

Saudara laki-laki dan perempuan seayah dan seibu

T: Seorang wanita meninggal dunia meninggalkan seorang suami, ibu, saudara perempuan kandung, serta seorang saudara laki-laki dan saudara perempuan seayah. Sebutkan bagian masing-masing dari warisan?

J: Pertanyaan ini terdiri dari sepuluh bagian, asalnya enam, namun menjadi sepuluh karena penerapan skema pembagian yang disebut 'Awl. Pertanyaan ini disebut "Dhat Al-Furukh" [Induk Para Anak Ayam,] karena banyaknya penggunaan skema 'Awl di dalamnya.

- Suami berhak atas setengah dari harta warisan,

- Ibu berhak atas seperenam sebagai bagiannya,

- Saudara perempuan kandung berhak atas tiga bagian,

- Saudara perempuan seayah berhak atas seperenam untuk melengkapi bagian dua pertiga bersama saudara perempuan kandung. Saudara laki-laki dan perempuan seibu berhak atas sepertiga sebagai dua bagian. Total dengan demikian adalah sepuluh bagian. Ini adalah berdasarkan ijma' para ulama Muslim.

Suami, ibu, dan saudara perempuan seibu

T: Seorang wanita meninggal dunia meninggalkan seorang suami, ibu, dan saudara perempuan seibu. Berapa bagian masing-masing dari warisan?

J: Pertanyaan ini dibagi menjadi sebelas bagian:

- Anak perempuan berhak atas enam bagian,
content_ar: A: Based on the fact that the الأب made the الهبة in the dower of his son’s الزوجة, he can in no الطريق return it. BEQUESTS A bequest or an acknowledgment of a debt? Q: A man in his الموت المرض said: “لذا and لذا sum of المال should be paid to the orphans of such and such. It was not clear whether his saying was intended to be an acknowledgement of a debt he owed or a bequest? A: إذا there was an indication in the context whether he intended an acknowledgement of a debt or a bequest, the deceased’s phrase should be interpreted in the light of such indication. إذا it cannot be distinguished, it will be considered as a bequest. Making bequests of unequal shares to one’s الأولاد Q: A man bequeathed unequal shares to his الأولاد and brought witnesses to attest such bequest in his deathbed. Can this bequest be effective or not? A: An ill person is not permitted to assign a bequest to some of his الأولاد, to be delivered in his lifetime or بعد his الموت, nor to acknowledge a sum of المال or property to one of his الأولاد. should he does an of the above, it will not be effective, إلا إذا بعد the consent of the remaining heirs. This is وفقاً لـ the consensus of مسلم العلماء. Being a witness on such kind of bequests is not permissible, as it is regarded as supporting an unfair cause. Such kind of bequests or gifts is considered one of the greatest الذنوب that bring about destruction to it their doer. This is لأن a bequest or a الهبة of such kind most likely leads to disagreement and even hatred and enmity among the heirs. Q: A woman. bequeathed one-ثالثاً of her property to her daughter قبل her الموت. The الأب of the girl then was made her guardian. The الأب then made a case at the court to prove such bequest. The court ascertained the الموت of the testatrix, her being bequeathed to her daughter, and that her الأب accepted to be a guardian of such bequest. The judge, غير أن, could not issue a judgment to prove the bequest لأن of the inability to let the young girl make an oath to this effect بسبب her minority. Should her الأب take an oath instead of her? Or should the judge postpone the issuance of the judgment حتى the girl attains majority to take the oath herself? A: The الأب should not take the oath instead of her daughter, nor should the judgment be postponed حتى the girl attains majority. Rather, the judgment should be issued immediately. There is not disagreement among العلماء on this point, لذا long as no legal claim is made against the الأب that opposes his claim. Annulment of a bequest Q: A woman made a bequest in her المرض to certain persons, بما في ذلك her الزوج and her الأخ. Long بعد, she gave birth to a male الولد, then died. Can the bequest she made be annulled in this case? A: All that exceeds one-ثالثاً of the bequest should be returned to the heirs. أما the bequest for the الزوج, it is not valid, as he is entitled to inheritance. أما the الأخ, the bequest for him is valid, as he cannot inherit in the presence of the son. وهكذا, the portion bequeathed to the الأخ and others should not exceed one-ثالثاً of the whole estate, and it is to be distributed among them وفقاً لـ the shares specified by the deceased. Can a nephew be an heir? Q: A woman died and left behind a nephew (son of her الأخت). She allocated more than one-ثالثاً of her estate as a الصدقة. Can this bequest be effected, وهكذا giving the nephew the rest of the estate? A: A bequest should not exceed one-ثالثاً out of the entire estate. More than one-ثالثاً is not permitted, إلا إذا بعد the consent of the legal heirs. A nephew is entitled to inherit the entire estate, وفقاً لـ the opinion of the العلماء who give the uterine relatives the الحق to inherit. This is the opinion of the majority of early المسلمون (السلف), Abu Hanifah, أحمد, a group of the Shafi’i العلماء, and a variant opinion in the المالكي School, in case the public treasury is deficient. A bequest to a الزوج and a paternal uncle and a grandmother Q: A woman died and left behind a الأب, a paternal uncle and a grandmother. He الأب had claimed her majority at the court قبل he gave her in marriage. In her الموت المرض, she bequeathed one-half of her estate to her الزوج and the other one-half to her paternal uncle, giving nothing to her الأب and grandmother. Is this bequest valid? A: The bequest for the paternal uncle is valid, but should not exceed one-ثالثاً, except with the consent of other heirs. The bequest for the الزوج, غير أن, is totally invalid, except إذا the heirs accepted it. إذا the heirs rejected the bequest of more than one-ثالثاً to the paternal uncle, the الزوج will be entitled to half of the estate بعد deducting the portion bequeathed, i.e. one-ثالثاً. The grandmother and the الأب will be entitled to one-سادساً each. A bequest of الحج Q: Five days prior to her الموت, a woman bequeathed some things, بما في ذلك الحج, التلاوة of the Holy Qur’an. الصدقة, etc. Is her bequest applicable? A: إذا she bequeathed that one-ثالثاً of her estate he dedicated to finance certain acts of devotion to pleasc الله, her bequest should be fulfilled, even in her الموت المرض. إذا the bequest exceeds one-ثالثاً of the whole estate, the increase should be excluded, except إذا the heirs consent. إذا the bequest is made for anything other than the acts of devotion, it will not be executable. Benefit for the deceased Q: A man bequeathed قبل his الموت that his الزوجة should not give المال to whomever recites Qur’an on his القبر or recite Qur’anic الآيات and then dedicate them to him, claiming that his القلب was already full of Qur’an and in need of no more. The الزوجة knew that he could have not memorized the Holy Qur’an. Can his bequest be effective? The الزوجة’s purpose is to give المال to any needy person in return for reciting Qur’anic الآيات and dedicating them to her الزوج. Is she permitted to do لذا? A: The bequest of the الزوج should be fulfilled, بما أن the gratuity in return for التلاوة of the Qur’an on the القبر ina religious البدعة that should be avoided. It is only acceptable إذا made for free. This can be elaborated as follows: 1. Hiring someone to recite Qur’anic الآيات and dedicate them to the deceased is not acceptable, and no الأجر of that will reach the deceased. 2. Hiring someone to recite Qur’anic الآيات only is أيضاً a religious البدعة. 3. Hiring someone as an _ instructor of the Holy Qur’an is permissible. إذا that woman desires to benefit her deceased الزوج, she may give الصدقة on his behalf, as the الأجر of الصدقة reaches the deceased, وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of العلماء. إذا she gives الصدقة to a group of poor reciters to fulfill their needs and enable them to give up the profession of reciting Qur’an on the graves, her الصدقة will be rewardable and such الأجر will reach the deceased. The guardian of the orphan girl Q: An orphan girl has a الثروة, and a person is proposing to her. Can her guardian sell some of her property to buy her the requirements of marriage that suits her social status? A: Yes, the guardian can sell some of her property to buy her all necessities of the wedding on equitable terms. THERIFANCE The share of the widow Q: What is the share of the widow of the inheritance of her deceased الزوج, knowing that he left behind الأولاد? A: The widow is entitled to her dower and all liabilities the الزوج owed her, just like other creditors. بعد deducting the amounts of debt and executable bequest out of the total of the estate, the الزوجة is entitled to one-ثامناً of the estate بسبب the presence of the الأولاد. The share of the الزوج from the inheritance of his deceased الزوجة Q: A woman died leaving behind a الزوج and two الوالدان. The الأب seized her estate, claiming that his deceased daughter was not major. Is the الزوج entitled to inherit her? A: The estate of the deceased الزوجة is to be distributed as follows: - One-half for the الزوج, - One ثالثاً for the الأب, and - One-سادساً for the الأم. This is وفقاً لـ the view of the Four Juristic Schools, no matter she was major or not. How to distribute the estate? Q: Give each of the following heirs his/her share of inheritance: A الزوج, grandmother, full-brothers and a son? - The الزوج is entitled to one-رابعاً, - The grandmother is entitled to one-سادساً, - The son is entitled to the remaining portion of the estate, - The full brothers receive nothing. The sisters and the daughters Q: A woman died leaving behind a الزوج, two daughters, a الأم, and two full-sisters. Are the sisters entitled to a share of inheritance? A: the question is to be divided as follows: - The الزوج is entitled to one-رابعاً, - The الأم is entitled to one-سادساً, - The two daughters are entitled to two-thirds, - The full-sisters will receive nothing in the presence of the daughters. This is وفقاً لـ the consensus of the Four Juristic Schools. Paternal and maternal brothers and sisters Q: A woman died leaving behind a الزوج, الأم, fullsister and a_ paternal الأخ and الأخت. Give each his/her share of inheritance? A: The question consists of ten shares, originally six, but turns to ten by الفضيلة of the application of the scheme of distribution called ‘Awl. The question is called “Dhat AlFurukh” [the الأم of the Chicks,] بسبب the لذا many resort to the scheme of ‘Awl in it. - The الزوج is entitled to half of the estate, - The الأم is entitled to one-سادساً as a share, - The full-الأخت is entitled to three shares, - The paternal الأخت is entitled to one-سادساً من أجل complete the share of the two-thirds with the fullsister. The two maternal الأخ and الأخت are entitled to one-ثالثاً as two shares. The total وهكذا is ten shares. This is وفقاً لـ the consensus of the مسلم العلماء. A الزوج, الأم and maternal الأخت Q: A woman died leaving behind a الزوج, الأم, and maternal الأخت. What is the share of each of inheritance? A: This question is to be divided into eleven shares: - The daughter is entitled six shares,
